diagrams.title: Class Structure with Access Modifiers
diagrams.description: A box diagram showing the internal structure of a C++ class divided into three sections: public, protected, and private. This helps learners understand encapsulation and data hiding principles.
elements: Rectangle container with rounded corners representing the class
Three horizontal divisions with labels: 'public:', 'protected:', 'private:'
Sample member variables and methods in each section (e.g., name, age in public; salary in protected; password in private)
Lock icons or visual indicators showing different access levels
Class name header at top: 'class Person'
Color coding: green for public, yellow for protected, red for private
diagrams.purpose: Explains the three access modifiers (public, private, protected) and how they control visibility of class members

diagrams.title: Class to Object Instantiation
diagrams.description: A visual representation showing a class as a blueprint/template on the left, with arrows pointing to multiple object instances on the right, demonstrating the instance relationship.
elements: Single class box on the left labeled 'Class: Car' with attributes (color, model, speed) and methods (accelerate, brake)
Three object boxes on the right, each labeled 'Object' with specific values
First object: car1 with color='red', model='Toyota', speed=0
Second object: car2 with color='blue', model='Honda', speed=50
Third object: car3 with color='green', model='Ford', speed=100
Arrows from class to each object labeled 'instantiate' or 'create'
Dotted box around all objects labeled 'Object Instances'
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the difference between a class (blueprint) and objects (instances) in C++

diagrams.title: Access Modifier Scope Visualization
diagrams.description: Concentric circles diagram showing the scope of each access modifier - private being the innermost restricted circle, protected in the middle, and public as the outermost accessible circle.
elements: Three concentric circles centered on a class representation
Innermost circle labeled 'private' with red color - only accessible within the class itself
Middle circle labeled 'protected' with yellow color - accessible within class and derived classes
Outermost circle labeled 'public' with green color - accessible from anywhere
Class name in the center of circles
Legend or key explaining each access level
Example text showing which code can access each level (e.g., 'Main function' for public, 'Derived class' for protected)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the accessibility hierarchy of C++ access modifiers in a clear, intuitive manner
